user_query:
I run a weekly Mentorship Monday Q&A for junior players. Please use the local files in input/ to help me produce two things I can share and reuse.

Inputs:
- input/fan_questions.csv (Q&A engagement data; use it to compute metrics)
- input/draft_post.md (a rough draft of a guidance post that needs rewriting)

Deliverables:
1) outputs/engagement_summary.md
   - Compute and summarize engagement for the last 14 days relative to the latest date present in input/fan_questions.csv (i.e., use max(date) in the file as the end date; include both endpoints).
   - Include these sections and fields:
     a) Overview (last 14 days):
        - Reference window: START_DATE to END_DATE
        - Total questions
        - Questions answered (count) and answer rate (as a percentage with one decimal place)
        - Unique askers (count)
        - Average likes per question (one decimal)
        - Average likes for answered vs unanswered (one decimal each)
     b) Category breakdown (last 14 days): for each category with at least 1 question, list question_count and average_likes (one decimal). Sort this list by average_likes descending.
     c) Top 5 questions by likes (last 14 days): list each item as ID, category, likes, in descending order of likes.

2) outputs/rewritten_post.md
   - Rewrite input/draft_post.md into a polished, first‑person Mentorship Monday post aimed at young players (250–350 words). Clean up phrasing, remove bracketed notes/duplications, keep the advice practical and encouraging.
   - Include a short “By the numbers” paragraph after the opening paragraph that pulls from your computed metrics for the same 14‑day window. It must explicitly mention: total questions, answered count and answer rate, and the top 3 categories by average likes among categories with at least 2 questions, with their average likes (one decimal for each).
   - Retain and clearly cover these three principles from the draft: breathing routine, pre‑point decision, accept the result (include each phrase at least once).
   - End with a simple call to action inviting readers to send their questions for next week (no links needed).

Notes:
- Work entirely locally. Do not use the internet.
- Create the outputs/ folder if it doesn’t exist.
- Ensure all numbers in outputs/engagement_summary.md and the “By the numbers” paragraph in outputs/rewritten_post.md are consistent with input/fan_questions.csv and the specified 14‑day window. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
